actions.policy: Declarative commands use validated targets and governed navigation.

ai.policy: Model content renders as bounded text; privileged execution remains server-side.

charts.policy: SVG output uses governed sinks and remote/drilldown URLs use centralized policy.

components.policy: Components construct DOM or consume explicitly trusted server markup and safe targets.

core.policy: Lifecycle and registry orchestration does not accept remote authority.

css.policy: Static CSS distribution has no executable trust boundary.

dashboard.policy: Custom widget HTML is explicitly caller-trusted; ordinary values render as text.

desktop.policy: Body HTML is caller-trusted; links and versioned preference storage are governed.

dom.policy: Central sanitizer, Trusted Types sink, selector containment, and URL policy.

editor.policy: HTML is sanitized at every edit boundary; autosave/upload authority remains server-side.

effects.policy: Animation and effect targets tolerate malformed selectors and own lifecycle state.

extension-kit.policy: Extension profile helpers expose browser UI metadata without privileged execution.

forms.policy: Actions use URL policy; errors are bounded text; server validation remains authoritative.

icons.policy: First-party SVG definitions render through governed DOM sinks.

mcp.policy: Tool payloads are bounded text-only review UI; execution and audit remain server-side.

mobile.policy: Mobile shell behavior owns local DOM lifecycle without remote authority.

net.policy: Fetch/upload cancellation, retries, errors, credentials, and connectors are governed.

overlays.policy: Overlay ownership and focus targets remain local DOM operations.

push.policy: Subscription APIs require permission and server endpoints remain same-origin.

pwa.policy: Worker scope, cache eligibility, updates, and idempotent offline work are bounded.

query.policy: String insertion is explicitly trusted; selector operations use DOM ownership rules.

rad-adapter.policy: Versioned bounded envelopes apply trusted partials and allowlisted UI actions only.

realtime.policy: Endpoints, payload size, reconnect attempts, visibility, and teardown are bounded.

router.policy: Guards, same-origin partials, stale cancellation, history, and trusted HTML are governed.

state.policy: Versioned bounded persistence reports parse, schema, quota, and access failures.

table.policy: Remote payload shape/render limits and trusted-row HTML boundaries are explicit.
